query: Our codebase has isAdmin() checks scattered across 14 route files, 3 policy files, and 2 Blade templates.
expected_behavior: Identifies this as permission-model drift risk: role checks scattered across multiple layers
Names the exact business consequence: when a new admin role is added, 19 files must be updated and any miss creates a security hole
Recommends centralizing access decisions into a capability system as the architectural fix
Does not recommend simply adding more role checks in the existing pattern
Classifies this as a P1 Auth Integrity finding, not a P3 Maintainability finding
